title: thymus serpyllum subsp. bracteatus
intro: Thymus serpyllum subsp. bracteatus, also known as creeping thyme, is a low-growing, spreading perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial, antifungal, and antiseptic properties, which make it useful in treating wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of creeping thyme include its ability to aid in digestion, relieve stress, and reduce inflammation, making it a popular choice for herbal remedies. Creeping thyme can be prepared as a tea, essential oil, or infused oil, and is often used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and improve overall well-being.
